cards.back: A linear data structure where each element is a separate object, and each element (or node) points to the next node in the sequence.
A collection of data elements, each of which points to the next element in the sequence, allowing for efficient insertion and deletion of elements.
To represent a linked list in C, we use a struct to define the node and then create a pointer to the head of the list.
A stack implemented using a linked list, where the top element is stored in the last node and new elements are added at the front.
A queue implemented using a linked list, where elements are added to the end of the list (tail) and removed from the front of the list (head).
